new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) in containers > 2 L (subheading 2204 29 28)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.) in Behältnissen > 2 L (Unterposition 2204 29 28)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) in accordance with Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres (falling under heading 2204 29).

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 29 28 captured by the axioms are: (1) the wine bears a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) status per Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 — modelled via the existing ProtectedDesignationOfOriginOrGeographicalIndicationQuality quality class; (2) the wine is presented in containers holding more than 2 litres — modelled via the existing LargeContainer class (≥ 2 L); (3) the wine is not semi-sparkling — modelled via the complement restriction on SemiSparklingPressureQuality. A partial gap exists: (a) the specific alcohol-by-volume range that may apply under Additional Note 6(a) is not explicitly modelled here since the note cross-references sub-criteria not fully reproduced in the supplied legal text; (b) the LargeContainer TBox class is defined as ≥ 2 L (minInclusive 2.0) whereas the actual boundary for heading 2204 29 is strictly > 2 L — the TBox SmallContainer/LargeContainer boundary uses maxExclusive 2.0 / minInclusive 2.0 which effectively excludes exactly 2 L from the small container but includes it in LargeContainer, which is acceptable for modelling purposes. The distinction between PDO subheadings 22 (≤10 L) and 29 (>10 L vs. >2 L) within the 2204 29 group cannot be refined further without additional TBox container-volume individuals.
